title: AgentProfileDef
description: An agent profile is the whole personality of an NPC type (plan/04 §11): brain tier, sensor calibration, behavior content, movement. Tiered brains are the governing rule (the F.E.A.R. rat problem, ch07 §7.1): "fsm" for simple agents, "schedules" for deliberative ones; M4b–M4d add "bt", "goap", and "htn".

description.description: One line for humans and LLMs (surfaced by the manifest).

inherits.description: Parent def ID (same kind); objects deep-merge, scalars override, arrays replace or take {"$append"/"$remove"} patches.

entities.description: Entity template IDs this profile attaches to.

schedules.description: Schedule def IDs in priority order, highest first (brain = "schedules").

thinkInterval.description: Seconds between brain ticks; 0 = think every simulation tick (ch06 §6.9 tick-rate decoupling).

needs.description: Need def IDs this agent tracks (decayed per tick; drive the utility selector).

activities.description: Activity def IDs the PerformActivity task chooses among.

actions.description: GOAP action def IDs — the F.E.A.R. action-subset pattern (brain = "goap").

costProfile.description: Cost profile def ID overriding action costs (personality as data).

goalHysteresis.description: A new goal must beat the active one's priority by this margin (ch07 §7.4 oscillation guard).

rootTask.description: Root HTN task ID, compound or primitive (brain = "htn").

htnInterrupt.description: Condition names whose appearance forces an HTN re-decomposition.

passport.description: Group-compatibility tag for collective recycling (HZD Part 4).

metaSensors.description: Meta-sensor def IDs watching player behavior patterns on behalf of this agent.

flagConditions.description: Condition name → global flag key: truthy flags set the bit each update ("IS_NIGHT": "is_night").

hostileTags.description: Tags that mark entities as threats to this agent.

alertDecaySeconds.description: Seconds without threat before Alert decays back to Idle.

patrolPoints.description: Patrol route waypoints ([x,y,z] each) for the patrol_point move target.
